summary: Establishes the theoretical framework for machine learning: parametric vs nonparametric methods, the bias-variance tradeoff, and why models overfit.
keyIdeas: Parametric methods assume a fixed functional form; nonparametric methods let data determine the shape
The bias-variance tradeoff is the central tension in all ML: simple models underfit, complex models overfit
Training error always decreases with complexity but test error has a U-shape
Cross-validation provides honest estimates of how models perform on unseen data
Overfitting means the model memorized noise instead of learning patterns
practicalIntuition: Every ML model you ever build will face the bias-variance tradeoff — understanding it prevents you from shipping models that fail in production.
commonConfusion: Thinking a model with 99% training accuracy is good — it might be massively overfitting.
businessMeaning: Choosing between simple interpretable models and complex accurate ones is a daily decision in business analytics.

summary: Covers the practical toolkit of supervised learning: kNN, classification tuning, and rigorous model validation through cross-validation, bootstrap, and grid search.
keyIdeas: kNN classifies by majority vote of the k nearest training points — simple but effective
Grid search systematically evaluates hyperparameter combinations
k-fold cross-validation partitions data into k folds and rotates the test set
Bootstrap resampling estimates model stability with ~36.8% out-of-bag rate
Unsupervised methods (K-means, DBSCAN, hierarchical) discover structure without labels
practicalIntuition: Model validation is where most real-world ML projects succeed or fail — a model that isn't properly validated is a liability, not an asset.
commonConfusion: Confusing bootstrap with cross-validation: bootstrap samples WITH replacement (~36.8% OOB), k-fold partitions WITHOUT replacement into non-overlapping folds.
assignmentAnchor: Assignment 2: kNN for Regression and Classification (Ames Housing + Telco Churn)
businessMeaning: Proper validation prevents deploying models that look good in development but fail on real customers.

summary: Covers decision trees, and the ensemble methods that make them powerful: bagging reduces variance, random forest decorrelates trees, and boosting sequentially corrects errors.
keyIdeas: CART splits data recursively using Gini impurity or information gain
Single trees overfit easily — ensembles fix this
Bagging trains trees on bootstrap samples and averages predictions (variance reduction)
Random forest adds feature randomization to decorrelate trees beyond bagging
Boosting (GBM, AdaBoost.M5, XGBoost) fits sequential models to residuals, reducing bias
practicalIntuition: Tree ensembles (especially XGBoost) win most tabular data competitions and are the workhorse of industry ML — if you learn one method family deeply, make it this one.
commonConfusion: Confusing bagging and boosting: bagging trains trees independently in parallel (variance reduction), boosting trains trees sequentially on previous errors (bias reduction).
businessMeaning: Random forest and XGBoost are the default choices for credit scoring, fraud detection, churn prediction, and most business classification problems.

summary: Introduces artificial neural networks, gradient descent optimization, and the deep learning architectures (CNN, RNN, LSTM) that power modern AI.
keyIdeas: Neural networks learn by adjusting weights through backpropagation and gradient descent
Batch, stochastic, and mini-batch gradient descent trade off computation speed vs stability
CNNs extract spatial features from images using convolutional filters
RNNs and LSTMs process sequential data by maintaining hidden state across time steps
Transformers use attention mechanisms to process sequences without recurrence — enabling LLMs
practicalIntuition: Deep learning powers the AI revolution but requires large data and compute — know when traditional ML is sufficient and when deep learning adds real value.
commonConfusion: Thinking you always need deep learning — for tabular business data, XGBoost often outperforms neural networks.
businessMeaning: Image recognition, NLP, recommendation systems, and language models all rely on deep learning — understanding the fundamentals is essential for AI strategy roles.
